SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.operations

Class OperatingTrialAssessment

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.operations.OperatingTrialAssessment
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class OperatingTrialAssessment extends Object implements Serializable
Assesses a historian setpoint trial and checks whether its evidence supports a causal before/after comparison for process-model optimization.

The class collects time-stamped signal observations, a single defined intervention (a setpoint change), and an optional set of confounder gates. assess(double, double, double, double) computes the descriptive before/after effect for every signal and evaluates three kinds of quality gate:

  • Intervention - the observed setpoint signal must actually move from the old to the new value.
  • Data quality - each signal must have enough good-quality samples in both windows.
  • Confounder - each configured confounder signal must not move more than its allowed normalized change.

A causal interpretation is only declared defensible when all gates pass; otherwise the result is labelled DESCRIPTIVE. This mirrors ISO 14224-style source and data-quality traceability and deliberately makes no claim of a formal causal-inference standard. The class orchestrates plain time-series evidence and does not depend on any specific NeqSim equipment, so histories can come from OperationalTagMap reads, imported tagreader data, or simulated results.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • OperatingTrialAssessment

      public OperatingTrialAssessment(String studyName)
      Creates a trial assessment.
      Parameters:
      studyName - study or operating-case name

  • Method Details

    • addSignal

      public OperatingTrialAssessment addSignal(String name, double time, double value, String status, String unit)
      Adds a single time-stamped observation for a signal.

      Call this repeatedly to build up a signal time series. Samples do not need to be added in time order; they are sorted logically at assessment time by their window membership.

      Parameters:
      name - signal name, for example a logical tag or historian tag
      time - observation time in consistent units, typically seconds or epoch seconds
      value - observed value
      status - data-quality status; a null or empty value, or good, ok, or valid (case-insensitive) is treated as good quality, anything else as bad quality
      unit - engineering unit for the signal; the first non-empty unit provided for a signal is retained
      Returns:
      this assessment for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the signal name is null or empty

    • setIntervention

      public void setIntervention(String setpointName, double oldValue, double newValue, double transitionTime)
      Defines the intervention that the trial tests.
      Parameters:
      setpointName - name of the setpoint signal that was changed; a signal with this name should be added so the intervention gate can confirm the move
      oldValue - setpoint value before the change
      newValue - setpoint value after the change
      transitionTime - time at which the setpoint was changed
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the setpoint name is null or empty

    • addConfounder

      public void addConfounder(String signalName, double maxNormalizedChange)
      Registers a confounder gate.

      A confounder is a signal expected to stay approximately constant for the before/after comparison to be causally meaningful. If the signal's normalized change between the windows exceeds the allowed value the causal claim is rejected and the result stays descriptive.

      Parameters:
      signalName - name of the confounding signal
      maxNormalizedChange - maximum allowed absolute normalized change, for example 0.05 for five percent
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the signal name is null or empty, or the limit is negative or not finite

    • setMinGoodDataFraction

      public void setMinGoodDataFraction(double fraction)
      Sets the minimum fraction of good-quality samples required in each window.
      Parameters:
      fraction - minimum good-data fraction from 0 to 1
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the fraction is outside the range 0 to 1

    • assess

      public TrialAssessmentResult assess(double preWindowStart, double preWindowEnd, double postWindowStart, double postWindowEnd)
      Assesses the trial using a pre-intervention window and a post-intervention window.
      Parameters:
      preWindowStart - start time of the pre-intervention window, inclusive
      preWindowEnd - end time of the pre-intervention window, inclusive
      postWindowStart - start time of the post-intervention window, inclusive
      postWindowEnd - end time of the post-intervention window, inclusive
      Returns:
      the trial assessment result
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no intervention has been defined
      IllegalArgumentException - if a window is empty or the windows overlap

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the assessment configuration and the most recent result to schema-versioned JSON.
      Returns:
      JSON evidence document; when no assessment has run yet the result field is null

    • getLastResult

      public TrialAssessmentResult getLastResult()
      Returns the most recent assessment result.
      Returns:
      last result, or null when no assessment has run yet

    • buildEffect

      private TrialAssessmentResult.SignalEffect buildEffect(OperatingTrialAssessment.Signal signal, double preStart, double preEnd, double postStart, double postEnd)
      Builds the before/after effect for one signal.
      Parameters:
      signal - signal to evaluate
      preStart - pre-window start
      preEnd - pre-window end
      postStart - post-window start
      postEnd - post-window end
      Returns:
      signal effect

    • windowStatistics

      private OperatingTrialAssessment.WindowStatistics windowStatistics(OperatingTrialAssessment.Signal signal, double start, double end)
      Computes mean of good samples and good-data fraction inside a time window.
      Parameters:
      signal - signal to summarize
      start - window start, inclusive
      end - window end, inclusive
      Returns:
      window statistics

    • evaluateInterventionGate

      private boolean evaluateInterventionGate(Map<String,TrialAssessmentResult.SignalEffect> effectByName, List<TrialAssessmentResult.QualityGate> gates, List<String> warnings)
      Evaluates the intervention gate by confirming the setpoint moved as declared.
      Parameters:
      effectByName - signal effects keyed by name
      gates - gate list to append to
      warnings - warning list to append to
      Returns:
      true when the intervention is confirmed by data

    • evaluateDataQualityGates

      private boolean evaluateDataQualityGates(List<TrialAssessmentResult.SignalEffect> effects, List<TrialAssessmentResult.QualityGate> gates)
      Evaluates the per-signal data-quality gates.
      Parameters:
      effects - signal effects
      gates - gate list to append to
      Returns:
      true when every signal meets the minimum good-data fraction

    • evaluateConfounderGates

      private boolean evaluateConfounderGates(Map<String,TrialAssessmentResult.SignalEffect> effectByName, List<TrialAssessmentResult.QualityGate> gates, List<String> warnings)
      Evaluates the confounder gates.
      Parameters:
      effectByName - signal effects keyed by name
      gates - gate list to append to
      warnings - warning list to append to
      Returns:
      true when no confounder exceeds its allowed normalized change

    • isGood

      private static boolean isGood(String status)
      Classifies a data-quality status string as good or bad.
      Parameters:
      status - status string, may be null
      Returns:
      true when the status represents good quality
